mcqs.question: Which type of binding is used for function overloading in C++?
mcqs.explanation: Function overloading is resolved at compile time based on the function signature, so it uses early binding (static binding).

mcqs.question: What makes a function eligible for late binding?
mcqs.explanation: Declaring a function as virtual enables late binding. The function call is resolved at runtime using VTABLE lookup.

mcqs.question: Which of the following enables runtime polymorphism in C++?
mcqs.explanation: Virtual functions enable runtime polymorphism by allowing the derived class to override base class methods, resolved at runtime.

mcqs.question: What is VTABLE?

options.text: A lookup table for virtual functions

mcqs.correctAnswer: A lookup table for virtual functions
mcqs.explanation: VTABLE (Virtual Table) is a lookup table created by the compiler for classes with virtual functions, containing pointers to virtual function implementations.

mcqs.question: When is late binding performed?
mcqs.explanation: Late binding resolves function calls during program execution (runtime) based on the actual object type.

mcqs.question: What is a pure virtual function?
mcqs.explanation: A pure virtual function is declared using '= 0' syntax and has no implementation. It makes the class abstract.

mcqs.question: Can an abstract class be instantiated?
mcqs.explanation: An abstract class contains at least one pure virtual function and cannot be instantiated. Objects of such class cannot be created.

mcqs.question: Why should destructors be virtual in polymorphic hierarchies?

options.text: To ensure proper cleanup of derived objects

mcqs.correctAnswer: To ensure proper cleanup of derived objects
mcqs.explanation: Virtual destructors ensure that when a derived object is deleted through a base pointer, the derived destructor is also called for proper cleanup.

mcqs.question: What happens when object slicing occurs?
mcqs.explanation: Object slicing occurs when a derived object is assigned to a base object by value, losing the derived part and any virtual function behavior.

mcqs.question: Which keyword is used for runtime type identification?
mcqs.explanation: RTTI in C++ uses dynamic_cast for safe downcasting and typeid for obtaining type information at runtime.

mcqs.question: Early binding is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: Early binding is also called static binding because the function call is resolved during compilation based on the static type of the variable.

mcqs.question: Which binding is faster and why?

options.text: Early binding - resolved at compile time

mcqs.correctAnswer: Early binding - resolved at compile time
mcqs.explanation: Early binding is faster because function resolution happens at compile time, eliminating runtime overhead. Late binding requires VTABLE lookup at runtime.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of VPTR?
mcqs.explanation: VPTR (Virtual Table Pointer) is a hidden pointer in each object that points to the VTABLE of its class, used for runtime function resolution.

mcqs.question: Which operator is used for downcasting in polymorphic classes?
mcqs.explanation: dynamic_cast is used for safe downcasting in polymorphic classes (classes with virtual functions). It returns nullptr if cast fails.

mcqs.question: What is function overriding?

options.text: Same function name with same parameters
Same function name with different parameters
Derived class redefining base class virtual function

mcqs.correctAnswer: Derived class redefining base class virtual function
mcqs.explanation: Function overriding occurs when a derived class provides a new implementation for a virtual function inherited from the base class.

mcqs.question: Which of the following uses early binding?
mcqs.explanation: Function overloading uses early binding because the compiler resolves which overloaded function to call based on argument types at compile time.

mcqs.question: What is a virtual base class?

options.text: Base class with pure virtual function

mcqs.explanation: Virtual base class is used in virtual inheritance to avoid multiple copies of the same base class in inheritance hierarchies.

mcqs.question: How is runtime polymorphism achieved in C++?
mcqs.explanation: Runtime polymorphism is achieved through virtual functions when accessed via pointers or references to base class, allowing derived class methods to be called.

mcqs.question: Which is NOT a feature of late binding?
mcqs.explanation: Late binding is slower than early binding because function resolution happens at runtime through VTABLE lookup, adding overhead.

mcqs.question: What is the default binding for non-virtual functions in C++?
mcqs.explanation: Non-virtual functions in C++ use early binding by default. The compiler determines which function to call at compile time based on the declared type of the variable.
